title: Tennis Player Gets Angry and Loses Big Match
summary: Daniil Medvedev, a famous tennis player, had a very bad day at a tennis tournament in Monte Carlo. He lost a match 6-0, 6-0, which means he didn't win any games. This was the first time this happened in his career. During the match, he got so upset that he smashed his tennis racquet against the ground until it broke. The crowd watched and found it funny. Medvedev doesn't like playing on clay courts, which is the type of surface in this tournament. He made many mistakes and couldn't win points. His opponent, Matteo Berrettini, played very well and said it was one of his best matches. Berrettini will play another player next. Medvedev is known for getting angry in matches, like at the US Open last year.

background_read: Tennis is a popular sport played worldwide. Players compete in tournaments on different surfaces like grass, hard, and clay. Clay courts are slower and can make the ball bounce higher, which some players find tricky. Matches are scored in games and sets, with 6-0 meaning one player won all games. Players sometimes get frustrated and break their racquets, but this is against the rules and can lead to penalties. Daniil Medvedev is a top tennis player from Russia known for his skill and occasional temper. Matteo Berrettini is an Italian player who has been successful in big tournaments like Wimbledon.
Article_Structure: WHO: Daniil Medvedev, a tennis player, and Matteo Berrettini, his opponent
WHAT: Medvedev lost a match 6-0, 6-0 and smashed his racquet in anger
WHERE: At a tennis tournament in Monte Carlo
WHY: Because Medvedev dislikes clay courts and played poorly, while Berrettini played very well

perspectives.description: Medvedev got angry because he lost badly and doesn't like the clay surface, showing how emotions can affect performance.
